title: Indulgent Mexican Poblano Barbacoa
description: A rich and savory, crispy-skinned Mexican dish featuring roasted poblano peppers, melted stracciatella cheese, and tender beef.
instructions: Roast the poblano peppers on a baking sheet for 30 minutes, turning occasionally.
While the peppers are roasting, cook the ground beef in a large skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Once the peppers are done roasting, peel off the skin and chop them into a rough chop.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the ground beef and stir to combine.
Add the minced garlic to the ground beef and stir to combine.
Stir in the chopped poblano peppers.
Brush the corn tortillas with the olive oil and warm them in a dry skillet for a few seconds on each side.
Assemble the barbacoa by spooning the beef and pepper mixture onto a warmed tortilla, topping with melted stracciatella cheese, and serving immediately.
